seoTitle: CXMT DRAM Memory Products | DDR5 LPDDR5X | LiTong Distributor
seoDescription: Explore CXMT DRAM memory products including DDR5, LPDDR5X, DDR4 for servers, PCs, and mobile devices. Authorized distributor with technical support.

faqs.question: What are the main product categories offered by CXMT?
faqs.answer: CXMT offers four main DRAM product categories: (1) DDR5 Memory - high-performance DRAM with speeds up to 8000Mbps, available in 16Gb and 24Gb densities, supporting UDIMM, SODIMM, RDIMM, MRDIMM, CUDIMM, and CSODIMM form factors for data centers, servers, and PCs; (2) LPDDR5X Memory - ultra-low power mobile DRAM with speeds up to 10667Mbps, available in 12Gb and 16Gb densities for flagship smartphones and mobile devices; (3) DDR4 Memory - mainstream DRAM solutions for enterprise and consumer applications; (4) LPDDR4/LPDDR4X Memory - power-efficient mobile DRAM for smartphones, tablets, and IoT devices. All products are manufactured in CXMT's advanced 12-inch wafer fabs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review each product category to find the CXMT memory solution matching your application requirements, performance needs, and platform compatibility.

faqs.question: How do I select the right CXMT DDR5 memory for my server application?
faqs.answer: Selecting CXMT DDR5 for server applications requires considering: (1) Module type - RDIMM for standard servers, MRDIMM for high-performance servers, UDIMM for workstations; (2) Capacity - 16Gb or 24Gb dies, with modules ranging from 16GB to 96GB; (3) Speed grade - 4800Mbps, 5600Mbps, 6400Mbps, 7200Mbps, or 8000Mbps based on platform support; (4) Platform compatibility - verify with Intel Xeon or AMD EPYC processor specifications; (5) Form factor - standard height or TFF (Tall Form Factor) for high-capacity modules; (6) Temperature - standard (0°C to 85°C) or extended temperature range; (7) ECC support - required for server applications. CXMT DDR5 is fully JEDEC compliant and compatible with major server platforms.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use our DDR5 selection guide or contact technical team with your server platform specifications for recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the difference between CXMT DDR5 and DDR4 products?
faqs.answer: CXMT DDR5 vs DDR4 comparison: (1) Speed - DDR5 starts at 4800Mbps vs DDR4 at 3200Mbps, with DDR5 reaching 8000Mbps; (2) Voltage - DDR5 operates at 1.1V vs DDR4 at 1.2V, providing better power efficiency; (3) Density - DDR5 offers 16Gb and 24Gb dies vs DDR4 maximum 16Gb; (4) Architecture - DDR5 has dual 32-bit channels per DIMM vs DDR4 single 64-bit channel; (5) On-Die ECC - DDR5 includes ECC on the chip for improved reliability; (6) PMIC - DDR5 modules include on-board power management IC; (7) Compatibility - DDR5 requires new platforms (Intel 12th gen+, AMD AM5), not backward compatible with DDR4. CXMT offers both DDR5 for new designs and DDR4 for legacy systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose DDR5 for new high-performance designs. Use DDR4 for legacy platform compatibility. CXMT offers both options.

faqs.question: Which CXMT LPDDR5X product is suitable for my smartphone design?
faqs.answer: For smartphone LPDDR5X selection: (1) Density - 12Gb or 16Gb dies based on capacity requirements; (2) Speed grade - 8533Mbps for mainstream flagships, 9600Mbps for premium devices, 10667Mbps for ultra-premium; (3) Package type - standalone die for PoP (Package on Package) with SoC, or MCP (Multi-Chip Package) for integrated solutions; (4) Capacity configuration - 8GB, 12GB, 16GB, or 24GB based on device tier; (5) Power efficiency - all LPDDR5X speeds offer improved power consumption vs LPDDR5; (6) SoC compatibility - verify with mobile processor vendor (Qualcomm, MediaTek, Samsung); (7) Thermal characteristics - ensure meets smartphone thermal constraints. CXMT LPDDR5X is used in flagship smartphones from major brands.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our mobile memory specialists with your SoC platform and capacity requirements for LPDDR5X recommendations.

faqs.question: What memory form factors does CXMT support?
faqs.answer: CXMT supports comprehensive memory form factors: (1) DDR5 Modules - UDIMM (Unbuffered DIMM) for desktops, SODIMM (Small Outline DIMM) for laptops, RDIMM (Registered DIMM) for servers, MRDIMM (Multiplexed Rank DIMM) for high-performance servers; (2) Advanced DDR5 - CUDIMM/CSODIMM (Clocked Unbuffered DIMM) with integrated clock drivers for enhanced performance; (3) LPDDR Packages - standalone dies for PoP, MCP (Multi-Chip Package), and LPCAMM2 modules; (4) Custom Solutions - CXMT can provide custom packaging for specific customer requirements. All form factors are JEDEC compliant and compatible with standard memory slots and interfaces.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select form factor based on your system design. Contact technical team for custom packaging requirements.

categories.name: DDR5 Memory
categories.description: CXMT DDR5 DRAM delivers industry-leading performance with speeds up to 8000Mbps and densities up to 24Gb. Available in multiple module formats for data centers, enterprise servers, and high-performance PCs.
categories.longDescription: CXMT DDR5 memory represents the latest generation of DRAM technology, offering significant improvements in speed, capacity, and power efficiency over DDR4. As an authorized CXMT distributor, LiTong provides comprehensive DDR5 selection guidance, platform compatibility verification, and technical support. The product range includes 16Gb and 24Gb die densities with speeds from 4800Mbps to 8000Mbps. Multiple module types are available including UDIMM for desktops, SODIMM for laptops, RDIMM for servers, and MRDIMM for high-performance computing. CXMT DDR5 products are fully JEDEC compliant and compatible with Intel and AMD platforms.

series.name: DDR5-5600
series.description: Standard DDR5 for performance PCs and servers

series.description: Ultra-high speed DDR5 for enthusiast and HPC

selectionGuide.title: How to Select CXMT DDR5 Memory
selectionGuide.description: Consider platform compatibility, capacity requirements, speed grade, and module type when selecting CXMT DDR5.

faqs.question: What platforms are compatible with CXMT DDR5?
faqs.answer: CXMT DDR5 compatibility: (1) Intel platforms - 12th Gen Alder Lake, 13th Gen Raptor Lake, 14th Gen Raptor Lake Refresh, and Xeon Scalable processors; (2) AMD platforms - Ryzen 7000 series (AM5 socket), Ryzen 9000 series, and EPYC 9004/9005 processors; (3) Server platforms - Intel Xeon Scalable (Sapphire Rapids, Emerald Rapids), AMD EPYC (Genoa, Bergamo, Turin); (4) Verification - CXMT DDR5 is tested and validated on major platforms; (5) JEDEC compliance - ensures broad compatibility with DDR5-compliant systems. Always verify specific platform memory support lists (QVL) for guaranteed compatibility. CXMT works closely with platform vendors to ensure optimal compatibility.
faqs.decisionGuide: Check platform QVL (Qualified Vendor List) for guaranteed compatibility. Contact technical team for platform-specific guidance.

faqs.question: What is the difference between RDIMM and UDIMM?
faqs.answer: RDIMM vs UDIMM comparison: (1) RDIMM (Registered DIMM) - includes register chip that buffers address and command signals, enabling higher capacity and better signal integrity for servers; supports ECC; used in enterprise servers and workstations; (2) UDIMM (Unbuffered DIMM) - direct connection to memory controller, lower latency, lower cost; used in desktops and entry-level servers; typically no ECC (though ECC UDIMM exists); (3) Capacity - RDIMM supports higher capacities (up to 96GB per module), UDIMM typically up to 32GB; (4) Performance - UDIMM has slightly lower latency, RDIMM enables higher capacity; (5) Use case - RDIMM for servers, UDIMM for PCs and workstations. CXMT offers both RDIMM and UDIMM options.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use RDIMM for enterprise servers requiring high capacity. Use UDIMM for desktops and workstations.

faqs.question: How does DDR5 improve power efficiency?
faqs.answer: DDR5 power efficiency improvements: (1) Lower voltage - DDR5 operates at 1.1V vs DDR4 at 1.2V, reducing power consumption by ~10%; (2) On-Die Power Management - DDR5 includes PMIC (Power Management IC) on the module for better voltage regulation; (3) Improved architecture - dual 32-bit channels allow more granular power management; (4) Higher density - more memory capacity per module reduces number of modules needed; (5) Better performance-per-watt - higher bandwidth at similar power levels; (6) Temperature sensing - DDR5 modules include temperature sensors for thermal management. For data centers, DDR5 power savings translate to significant operational cost reductions and improved cooling efficiency.
faqs.decisionGuide: DDR5 provides 10-15% power savings vs DDR4. Important for data center TCO optimization.

faqs.question: What is MRDIMM and when should I use it?
faqs.answer: MRDIMM (Multiplexed Rank DIMM) explained: (1) Technology - uses data buffer to multiplex data from multiple ranks, effectively doubling bandwidth; (2) Speed - MRDIMM starts at 8800Mbps, with future speeds up to 12800Mbps+; (3) Use case - high-performance computing, AI/ML training, in-memory databases, high-frequency trading; (4) Platform support - Intel Xeon Scalable (Granite Rapids), AMD EPYC future generations; (5) Capacity - supports very high capacities for memory-intensive applications; (6) Cost - premium pricing for premium performance. MRDIMM is the next evolution after RDIMM for the most demanding server applications. CXMT offers MRDIMM and TFF MRDIMM for high-performance servers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use MRDIMM for HPC, AI/ML, and memory bandwidth-intensive applications. Requires latest server platforms.

faqs.question: What is the maximum capacity CXMT DDR5 supports?
faqs.answer: CXMT DDR5 capacity options: (1) Die density - 16Gb and 24Gb DDR5 dies available; (2) Module capacity - using 24Gb dies: UDIMM up to 48GB, SODIMM up to 48GB, RDIMM up to 96GB; (3) 3D stacking - future products may support higher densities through advanced packaging; (4) System capacity - depends on number of memory channels and slots; example: 2-socket server with 12 channels and 96GB RDIMM = 1.15TB per socket; (5) TFF MRDIMM - Tall Form Factor enables higher capacity in space-constrained designs. The 24Gb density is particularly valuable for data centers seeking maximum memory capacity per server.
faqs.decisionGuide: 24Gb dies enable highest capacity. Choose based on system memory slot limitations and capacity requirements.

products.name: DDR5-8000 RDIMM Server Memory
products.shortDescription: 24Gb DDR5 RDIMM with 8000Mbps speed for high-performance servers and data centers.
products.description: High-performance DDR5 RDIMM with 24Gb density and 8000Mbps speed for enterprise servers.
descriptionParagraphs: The CXMT DDR5-8000 RDIMM delivers cutting-edge memory performance for demanding data center applications.
Featuring 24Gb die density and 8000Mbps transfer rate, this module provides exceptional bandwidth for AI, HPC, and cloud computing.
The registered design with ECC support ensures data integrity and signal stability in high-capacity server configurations.

faeReview.author: Dr. Li Wei
faeReview.title: Senior Memory Architect
faeReview.content: The CXMT DDR5-8000 RDIMM represents the pinnacle of server memory technology. The 24Gb density is a game-changer for data centers - you can achieve 1.5TB+ per socket with standard 2-socket servers. The 8000Mbps speed provides the bandwidth needed for AI training workloads. I've tested these modules on Intel Xeon Scalable platforms and the performance is exceptional. The signal integrity at 8000Mbps is solid thanks to the registered design. One important consideration: ensure your server platform supports 8000Mbps - not all DDR5 platforms do. For AI and HPC applications, this is currently the best memory option available from CXMT. The power efficiency at 1.1V also helps with data center cooling.
faeReview.highlight: 24Gb density and 8000Mbps speed ideal for AI and HPC applications

alternativeParts.reason: CXMT offers equivalent performance at competitive pricing

companionParts.description: Recommended companion component for optimal system performance

faqs.question: What server platforms support DDR5-8000?
faqs.answer: DDR5-8000 platform support: (1) Intel - Xeon Scalable (Emerald Rapids and later), 6th/7th Gen Xeon; (2) AMD - EPYC 9005 series (Turin), future EPYC generations; (3) Verification - check platform QVL for specific module compatibility; (4) BIOS - may require BIOS update for 8000Mbps support; (5) Cooling - ensure adequate cooling for high-speed operation. Not all DDR5 platforms support 8000Mbps - many are limited to 4800-6400Mbps. Verify platform specifications before ordering. CXMT provides platform compatibility lists for reference.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify platform supports 8000Mbps. Check QVL for specific module compatibility.

faqs.question: How does 24Gb density benefit my server deployment?
faqs.answer: 24Gb density benefits: (1) Higher capacity per module - 48GB vs 32GB for 16Gb dies; (2) Fewer modules needed - achieve target capacity with fewer DIMMs; (3) Cost savings - potentially lower cost per GB; (4) Power efficiency - fewer modules consume less power; (5) Future-proofing - higher density for expanding workloads; (6) AI/ML advantage - larger models fit in memory. Example: Server with 12 memory channels. Using 16Gb dies: 12 × 32GB = 384GB. Using 24Gb dies: 12 × 48GB = 576GB. 50% more capacity with same number of slots. Critical for memory-intensive applications like AI training.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 24Gb for maximum capacity. Beneficial for AI/ML and memory-intensive workloads.

products.name: DDR5-5600 UDIMM Desktop Memory
products.shortDescription: 16Gb DDR5 UDIMM with 5600Mbps speed for desktop PCs and workstations. Ideal for industrial and consumer applications ...
products.description: High-performance DDR5 UDIMM for desktop PCs and workstations with 5600Mbps speed.
descriptionParagraphs: The CXMT DDR5-5600 UDIMM delivers excellent performance for desktop PCs and workstations.
With 16Gb density and 5600Mbps speed, this module provides significant performance uplift over DDR4.
The unbuffered design offers low latency ideal for gaming and content creation applications.

faeReview.author: Zhang Ming
faeReview.title: PC Hardware Specialist
faeReview.content: The CXMT DDR5-5600 UDIMM hits the sweet spot for mainstream DDR5 builds. 5600Mbps is supported by virtually all DDR5 platforms including Intel 12th/13th/14th gen and AMD Ryzen 7000/9000 series. The 16Gb density provides good capacity options - 16GB for entry-level, 32GB for mainstream, 64GB for power users. I've tested these with both Intel and AMD platforms and compatibility has been excellent. The modules run stable at JEDEC 5600 speeds without any tuning needed. For gamers and content creators, this provides a noticeable performance boost over DDR4-3200. One tip: if your motherboard supports XMP 3.0, you can potentially run these at higher speeds, though 5600 is plenty fast for most applications. In my professional experience, I highly recommend this CXMT memory device for high-performance applications where reliability is critical. I suggest implementing proper thermal management and considering the complete system requirements for optimal performance. Based on field feedback, this device consistently delivers excellent results.
faeReview.highlight: 5600Mbps sweet spot for mainstream DDR5 builds with broad platform compatibility

faqs.question: Is DDR5-5600 backward compatible with slower DDR5 platforms?
faqs.answer: DDR5 speed compatibility: (1) Downclocking - DDR5 modules automatically downclock to platform's maximum supported speed; (2) Example - DDR5-5600 module in 4800Mbps platform runs at 4800Mbps; (3) JEDEC standard - all speeds are JEDEC standardized for interoperability; (4) No performance gain - module won't run faster than platform supports; (5) Future upgrade - buy faster module now for future platform upgrade. This means you can safely buy DDR5-5600 even if your current platform only supports 4800Mbps. The module will work at 4800Mbps today and run at full 5600Mbps when you upgrade to a faster platform.
faqs.decisionGuide: DDR5 modules are backward compatible. Buy faster modules for future platform upgrades.

categories.name: LPDDR5X Memory
categories.description: CXMT LPDDR5X delivers ultra-high speed mobile memory up to 10667Mbps with exceptional power efficiency. Designed for flagship smartphones, tablets, and AI edge devices.
categories.longDescription: CXMT LPDDR5X represents the cutting edge of mobile memory technology, offering speeds up to 10667Mbps with industry-leading power efficiency. As an authorized CXMT distributor, LiTong provides comprehensive LPDDR5X selection guidance, platform compatibility verification, and technical support. The product range includes 12Gb and 16Gb die densities with multiple speed grades. Available in standalone dies for PoP (Package on Package), MCP (Multi-Chip Package), and LPCAMM2 module formats. CXMT LPDDR5X is used in flagship smartphones from major brands and delivers the performance needed for AI, gaming, and multitasking.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select CXMT LPDDR5X Memory
selectionGuide.description: Consider SoC compatibility, capacity requirements, speed grade, and package type when selecting CXMT LPDDR5X.

faqs.question: What is the difference between LPDDR5 and LPDDR5X?
faqs.answer: LPDDR5X improvements over LPDDR5: (1) Speed - LPDDR5X up to 10667Mbps vs LPDDR5 up to 6400Mbps, 67% bandwidth increase; (2) Voltage - LPDDR5X operates at 0.5V vs LPDDR5 at 0.5V/0.9V, with improved voltage scaling; (3) Power efficiency - LPDDR5X offers ~20% better power efficiency; (4) Signal integrity - enhanced training and calibration; (5) Backward compatible - LPDDR5X devices work in LPDDR5 systems at LPDDR5 speeds; (6) Use case - LPDDR5X for premium flagships, LPDDR5 for mainstream. The performance improvement is significant for AI, gaming, and high-refresh displays. Most 2024-2025 flagship smartphones use LPDDR5X.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use LPDDR5X for premium devices requiring maximum performance. LPDDR5 sufficient for mainstream.

faqs.question: Which mobile SoCs support CXMT LPDDR5X?
faqs.answer: LPDDR5X SoC compatibility: (1) Qualcomm - Snapdragon 8 Gen 2/3, Snapdragon 7+ Gen 2 and later; (2) MediaTek - Dimensity 9200/9300/9400 series; (3) Samsung - Exynos 2400 and later; (4) Apple - A17 Pro and later (though Apple uses proprietary packaging); (5) Verification - CXMT LPDDR5X validated on major SoC platforms; (6) Speed support - verify SoC supports target LPDDR5X speed (8533/9600/10667). Most 2024 flagship Android phones use LPDDR5X. Check SoC datasheet for specific memory support. CXMT works with SoC vendors to ensure compatibility.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify SoC memory interface supports LPDDR5X target speed. Contact technical team for platform validation.

faqs.question: What package options are available for LPDDR5X?
faqs.answer: LPDDR5X package options: (1) Standalone die - for PoP (Package on Package) with SoC, most common for smartphones; (2) MCP (Multi-Chip Package) - LPDDR5X stacked with NAND or other dies; (3) LBGA - standard ball grid array package; (4) LPCAMM2 - module format for laptops and embedded systems; (5) Custom packages - CXMT can develop custom packaging for specific requirements; (6) Capacity options - 8GB, 12GB, 16GB, 24GB, 32GB configurations. PoP is most common for smartphones. LPCAMM2 is emerging for laptops as replacement for soldered LPDDR.
faqs.decisionGuide: PoP for smartphones. LPCAMM2 for laptops. MCP for integrated storage solutions.

faqs.question: How much LPDDR5X capacity do I need for my smartphone?
faqs.answer: Smartphone LPDDR5X capacity guidelines: (1) 8GB - entry-level flagships, adequate for standard use; (2) 12GB - mainstream flagships, good for multitasking and gaming; (3) 16GB - premium flagships, excellent for heavy multitasking, AI features; (4) 24GB - ultra-premium, for power users and future-proofing; (5) AI considerations - on-device AI models benefit from more memory; (6) Future trends - 12GB becoming minimum for flagships, 16GB+ for premium. Consider your target market segment and price point. More memory enables better multitasking, larger AI models, and longer device relevance.
faqs.decisionGuide: 12GB for mainstream flagships. 16GB for premium. 24GB for ultra-premium and AI-focused devices.

faqs.question: What are the power savings of LPDDR5X?
faqs.answer: LPDDR5X power efficiency: (1) Voltage scaling - operates at 0.5V for lower speeds, scaling up for higher speeds; (2) DVFS - Dynamic Voltage and Frequency Scaling for optimal efficiency; (3) Power savings - ~20% lower power vs LPDDR5 at same bandwidth; (4) Idle power - improved deep sleep modes; (5) Thermal - lower power dissipation reduces device temperature; (6) Battery life - contributes to longer battery life in mobile devices. For smartphones, LPDDR5X power savings translate to better battery life, especially during intensive tasks like gaming and AI processing. The efficiency gains are significant for thin-and-light devices with limited thermal budgets.
faqs.decisionGuide: LPDDR5X provides 20% power savings vs LPDDR5. Important for battery life and thermal management.

products.name: LPDDR5X-10667 Mobile Memory
products.shortDescription: 16Gb LPDDR5X with 10667Mbps speed for flagship smartphones and premium mobile devices.
products.description: Ultra-high speed LPDDR5X memory for flagship mobile devices with 10667Mbps performance.
descriptionParagraphs: The CXMT LPDDR5X-10667 delivers industry-leading mobile memory performance for flagship smartphones.
With 16Gb density and 10667Mbps speed, this memory enables seamless multitasking, AI processing, and high-refresh gaming.
The exceptional power efficiency ensures long battery life even under demanding workloads.

faeReview.author: Wang Xiaohong
faeReview.title: Mobile Memory Expert
faeReview.content: The CXMT LPDDR5X-10667 is the pinnacle of mobile memory technology. 10667Mbps provides the bandwidth needed for 2024-2025 flagship smartphones with AI features, 120Hz+ displays, and advanced camera systems. I've seen this memory in action on Snapdragon 8 Gen 3 and Dimensity 9300 platforms - the performance is exceptional. The 16Gb density enables 16GB configurations with dual dies, or 32GB with quad dies for ultra-premium devices. Power efficiency is excellent - devices using this memory show noticeably better battery life under load compared to LPDDR5. The validation work CXMT has done with major SoC vendors shows in the seamless compatibility. For any flagship smartphone design, this is the memory to beat. In my professional experience, I highly recommend this CXMT memory device for high-performance applications where reliability is critical. I suggest implementing proper thermal management and considering the complete system requirements for optimal performance. Based on field feedback, this device consistently delivers excellent results.
faeReview.highlight: 10667Mbps speed and 16Gb density ideal for AI-enabled flagship smartphones

alternativeParts.reason: CXMT offers flagship performance at competitive pricing

companionParts.description: Recommended companion component for optimal system performance

faqs.question: What is the maximum capacity with 16Gb LPDDR5X dies?
faqs.answer: 16Gb LPDDR5X capacity options: (1) Single die - 2GB per die (16Gb = 2GB); (2) Dual die - 4GB in dual-die package; (3) Quad die - 8GB in quad-die package; (4) Octa die - 16GB with 8 dies (2 stacks of 4); (5) Maximum - 32GB with 16 dies (4 stacks of 4, or advanced packaging). Typical smartphone configurations: 8GB (4 dies), 12GB (6 dies), 16GB (8 dies), 24GB (12 dies), 32GB (16 dies). The 16Gb density enables high capacities in space-constrained mobile devices. Advanced packaging techniques like stacked dies enable these high capacities in smartphone form factors.
faqs.decisionGuide: 16Gb dies enable up to 32GB in smartphones. Choose capacity based on device tier and use case.

faqs.question: How do I verify LPDDR5X compatibility with my SoC?
faqs.answer: LPDDR5X compatibility verification: (1) SoC datasheet - check memory interface specifications for LPDDR5X support and maximum speed; (2) Reference designs - use SoC vendor's reference schematics and layout guidelines; (3) SI simulation - perform signal integrity simulation for your specific PCB design; (4) Validation testing - test with actual silicon on your PCB; (5) Vendor support - engage CXMT and SoC vendor for technical support; (6) JEDEC compliance - ensure design meets JEDEC LPDDR5X specifications. Critical items: PCB layout (length matching, impedance control), power delivery (clean 0.5V supply), and signal integrity (minimal crosstalk). CXMT provides IBIS models and technical support for design validation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow SoC vendor reference design. Perform SI simulation. Engage CXMT for technical support.
